banco_oraciones.oracion: Sofía has got a laptop and two cameras.
I have got a bike, but I haven't got a car.
Ben has got a new phone. For now.
We have got a big family.
I haven't got my keys! Again!
Kenji hasn't got a guitar, but he wants one.
They haven't got an umbrella, and it's raining.
What have you got in your bag?
Have you got a bike? Yes, I have.
Has Ben got his keys? No, he hasn't.
Have they got a camera? Yes, they have.

interaccion: Ben: In Britain we say it like this: I**'ve got** a camera, I**'ve got** a plan, and I **haven't got** any money.
Sofía: So 'have got' = 'have'? I **have** a laptop... and I**'ve got** a laptop?
Ben: Exactly! Same meaning. But careful: **Have you got...?** — no 'do' in the question!
Abuela Carmen: I**'ve got** four grandchildren, two hands and one rule. **Has** anybody **got** questions? No? Dinner time.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: Do you have got a pen?

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: 'Have got' says what you own or possess — it means the same as 'have', but makes questions and negatives without 'do': Have you got...? I haven't got...

filas: Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't.
Has she got a dog? Yes, she has.
I've got a laptop = I have a laptop

ejercicios.pregunta: I ___ got an umbrella, and it's raining!

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Have got' means the same as 'have': I've got a bike = I have a bike.
The question with 'have got' uses 'do': 'Do you have got a pen?'

ejercicios.texto: Kenji ___ got a guitar, but he wants one. (negative)

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Do you have got a pen?

ejercicios.pregunta: Have you got a phone? (short answer)
Has your teacher got a helicopter? (short answer, No)

guion: Sofía: Trip checklist! Ben, have you got the tickets?
Ben: Yes, I have! And I've got my camera and my hat.
Sofía: Kenji, have you got the map?
Kenji: Yes! And I've got snacks for everybody.
Sofía: Perfect. Ben... have you got your keys?
Ben: ...I haven't got my keys. One moment, please.

reading.texto: Ben's British corner, episode one: 'Have got'. In London, my family says it all the time. My mum has got a garden with roses. My dad has got an old car — older than me. My sister has got three cats, and the cats have got strong opinions. Me? I've got a camera, a backpack, and a one-way ticket to adventure. I haven't got a lot of money, but I've got good friends here in Colombia — and Abuela Carmen says that a person with good friends has got everything.

preguntas.afirmacion: Ben's mum has got a garden.
His sister has got three dogs.
Ben has got a lot of money.
Abuela Carmen says a person with good friends has got everything.
